commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh Concerning the works of men, by the word of your lips I have kept myself from the paths of the destroyer. Augustine of Hippo 4. "That My mouth may not speak the works of men" (ver. That nothing may proceed out of My mouth, but what relates to Thy glory and praise; not to the works of men, which they do beside Thy will. "Because of the words of Thy lips. " Because of the words of Thy peace, or of Thy prophets. "I have kept hard ways. " I have kept the toilsome ways of human mortality and suffering.
